items.text: ATTENTION RESIDENTS — Elevator B in Henley Hall will be out of service from March 8 to March 12 for cable replacement. Please use Elevator A or the south stairwell. A $5 service credit will be applied to affected dorm accounts.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what compensation is provided to affected residents?

options.A: A free parking pass for one week
options.C: A discount on next semester's housing fee
options.D: A small service credit to their dorm account

questions.explanation: The notice states that a $5 service credit will be applied to affected dorm accounts.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Elevator A during the closure?

options.A: It will be reserved for staff only.
options.B: It will run on a reduced schedule.
options.C: It will remain available for resident use.
options.D: It will undergo similar cable replacement.

questions.explanation: The notice tells residents to use Elevator A as the alternative, implying it will remain available.

items.text: Hi everyone, our Photography Club meeting has moved from Wednesday at 6 PM to Thursday at 7 PM. We'll meet in Room 218 of Greer Hall instead of the usual lounge. Please bring your edited prints. Thanks, Lydia.

questions.stem: According to the email, when will the meeting take place?
questions.explanation: The email states the meeting has moved to Thursday at 7 PM.

questions.stem: The word "moved" in the email is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In context, the meeting time changing from Wednesday to Thursday means it was rescheduled.

items.text: Hi Janelle, just confirming our tutoring session for Friday at 3:00 PM in Room 112 of the Math Center. Please bring your problem set from Chapter 4. Let me know if you need to reschedule. Best, Marcus.

questions.stem: According to the email, where will the tutoring session take place?

options.B: In Room 112 of the Math Center

questions.explanation: The email explicitly states the session will be in Room 112 of the Math Center.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Janelle's preparation for the session?

options.A: She should review specific chapter material.
options.B: She should bring all course textbooks.
options.C: She should arrive thirty minutes early.
options.D: She should email her solutions in advance.

questions.explanation: Bringing the Chapter 4 problem set implies she should review that chapter's material before the session.

items.text: Dear Mr. Calderon, our records show your meal plan ended the semester with an unused balance of $42.50. Your refund will be deposited within five business days. Please reply if your bank details have changed. Thank you, Campus Dining.

questions.stem: According to the email, what is Mr. Calderon's unused meal plan balance?
questions.explanation: The email explicitly states an unused balance of $42.50.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Mr. Calderon's bank details?

options.A: They were submitted with his application.
options.C: They will be verified by phone.
options.D: They are already on file with the office.

questions.explanation: The email asks for a reply only if bank details have changed, implying current details are already on file.
